justification: All pass criteria met: Both stakeholders' interests are characterized fairly and in depth. Formal escalation is produced with explicit call for executive arbitration. No acceptance criteria are produced (the array is empty). Confidence level is 30, well below the 45 threshold. Options C/F are clearly labeled as recommendations requiring executive decision, not presented as resolutions.

observations.text: Excellent identification of the contradiction as a business strategy conflict, not a technical problem
Empty business_rules and acceptance_criteria arrays correctly reflect that nothing can be finalized until the conflict is resolved
Six concrete options presented, all clearly framed as proposals for executive decision, not as resolved requirements
Identified a missing stakeholder (Legal/Compliance) and elevated it as a separate escalation — goes beyond the rubric expectations
Edge cases are listed but each explicitly states 'Cannot define' due to the unresolved conflict, reinforcing that no behavior can be specified yet
Rejection reasons explicitly refuse both stakeholder positions as stated, demonstrating true neutrality
Confidence reasoning is thorough — cites the contradiction, missing stakeholder, and lack of quantitative data from either side
Business impact analysis fairly notes that both directions carry high risk, without privileging either side

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Real-time courier tracking feature for customer-facing delivery experience. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory requirements regarding courier location visibility. Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator; Operations demands zero location exposure due to measurable driver attrition. This is a foundational product decision that cannot be resolved by engineering — it requires executive arbit
